qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Malformed encrypted PEM missing DEK-Info triggers uncaught NullPointerException
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a pkix PEM parser null-dereference in `org.bouncycastle.openssl.PEMParser`, while the advisory patch adds AEAD chunk-size bounds checks in the bcpg OpenPGP path (`AEADEncDataPacket` and `PGPAEADUtil`) for pre-auth resource exhaustion.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.
evidence: GHSA-cj8j-37rh-8475 describes unbounded PGP AEAD chunk size handling in the `bcpg`/`pg` module that leads to pre-auth resource exhaustion.

The advisory fix commit `dc7530939ffb6cdb57636f3609d98e23b94e71c1` adds chunk-size range checks in `pg/src/main/java/org/bouncycastle/bcpg/AEADEncDataPacket.java` and `pg/src/main/java/org/bouncycastle/openpgp/operator/PGPAEADUtil.java`.

The only in-scope final qualifying finding is `pemparser_missing_dek_info_crash`, which targets `pkix/src/main/java/org/bouncycastle/openssl/PEMParser.java` and `JcaPKIXIdentityBuilder.build` and reproduces an uncaught `NullPointerException` from missing `DEK-Info` metadata.
That PEM parsing null-dereference is a different module, different parser state, and different failure mode from the advisory's AEAD chunk-size resource exhaustion path.
